evidence: The abstract states: "Most important variables for predicting susceptibility were time from admission to blood culture, patient age, hospital versus community-acquired infection, and age-adjusted weight score." Figure 5 reports that "the most important predictor for resistance to ceftriaxone is patient age (leaving this out would decrease the model accuracy 100% of the time). Patient age is closely followed by days from hospital admission to blood sample, age-adjusted weight score, and the classification of the infection as hospital- or community-acquired (omitting this variable would decrease model accuracy 75% of the time)." The paper uses ranger package random forests (default impurity-based importance), cites Janitza et al. for methodology, but reports no p-values or significance tests for importance scores. No corroboration via permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, conditional importance, or held-out validation is provided. The "days from admission" variable has exponential distribution (Table 1), making it susceptible to upward-biased impurity importance.
